_description: Template for your own golden self-test cases. Copy to golden-cases.json and add your validated closed PRs. Commander runs workers against these in dryrun mode and compares output. A regression means a commander prompt change made worker output meaningfully worse.
_how_to_add: After a manual Stage-0 validation of a closed PR in one of your repos, capture: repo, pr_num, parent_commit (pre-fix), key assertions (what the worker MUST produce), and minimum quality signals (tests added? no source regressions? build passes?).

_requires_legend.requires_docker: Skip the case if `docker info` fails (no docker daemon reachable). Used by cases that spin up localstack / test containers.
_requires_legend.requires_linear_mcp: Skip the case if the Linear MCP server isn't installed.
_requires_legend.requires_gh_auth: Skip the case if `gh auth status` fails.
_requires_legend.requires_claude_cli: Skip the case if `claude` CLI isn't on PATH (setup.sh hard-fails without it).
_requires_legend.requires_flyctl: Skip the case if `fly` (flyctl) isn't on PATH. Used by `fly config validate` checks against infra/fly.toml.
_requires_legend.requires_hadolint: Skip the case if `hadolint` isn't on PATH. Used for Dockerfile linting checks.

cases._comment: Commander-side fixture — not a PR regression. Validates scheduled autopickup's tick procedure against simulated queue/PAUSE/rate-limit states. Verifies quiet-by-default, yield-to-foreground, and 2-strike rate-limit auto-disable. See docs/specs/2026-04-16-scheduled-autopickup.md and CLAUDE.md 'Scheduled autopickup'.
cases.ticket_body_summary: Operator runs `autopickup every 5 min`. Simulate 3 consecutive ticks under different fixture conditions and verify commander-side behavior.

ticks._comment: Healthy queue; two ready tickets, no active workers, no pause, no rate-limit.
Rate-limit preflight fails — no spawn, increment consecutive_rate_limit_hits, stay quiet (this is the FIRST strike, not yet at the 2-strike threshold).
Second consecutive rate-limit preflight failure — hit the 2-strike auto-disable. enabled flips to false, ONE chat message surfaces, /loop exits.

yield_behavior_check._comment: Separate assertion: if operator types a message during a tick window, the tick must NOT race the foreground turn. Commander handles the operator first, resumes on next scheduled tick.
yield_behavior_check.expected: Tick #2 is deferred; 'status' gets a normal response; tick runs at next interval boundary.

Script-only self-test for scripts/ensure-repo-cloned.sh (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-cloud-repo-clone.md, ticket #73). Uses the public octocat/Hello-World repo so no GITHUB_TOKEN is required in CI; the unauthenticated HTTPS clone path is the same code path the cloud commander takes for public repos. Each step builds a fresh tmpdir fixture, runs the script, and asserts behavior via exit code + shell grep. Guardrails: bad-slug + missing-repo arguments both exit non-zero; first clone produces a .git dir; second run re-fetches without re-cloning (same SHA and same .git/config inode).

steps.name: bad slug (no slash) exits 1 with clear message
slug not in repos.json exits 1
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && printf '{"repos":[]}\n' > "$TMP/repos.json" && HYDRA_REPOS_JSON="$TMP/repos.json" scripts/ensure-repo-cloned.sh doesnotexist/doesnotexist 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo MISSING_OK

steps.name: first call clones octocat/Hello-World; second call re-fetches (same SHA, same .git inode)
steps.cmd: set -euo pipefail; TMP=$(mktemp -d); trap 'rm -rf "$TMP"' EXIT; printf '{"repos":[{"owner":"octocat","name":"Hello-World","local_path":"/tmp/unused","enabled":true,"ticket_trigger":"assignee","cloud_local_path":"%s/clone"}]}\n' "$TMP" > "$TMP/repos.json"; export HYDRA_REPOS_JSON="$TMP/repos.json"; export HYDRA_CLONE_DEPTH=5; scripts/ensure-repo-cloned.sh octocat/Hello-World >/dev/null 2>&1; test -d "$TMP/clone/.git" || { echo FAIL_NO_GIT; exit 1; }; sha1=$(git -C "$TMP/clone" rev-parse HEAD); inode1=$(ls -di "$TMP/clone/.git/config" | awk '{print $1}'); scripts/ensure-repo-cloned.sh octocat/Hello-World >/dev/null 2>&1; sha2=$(git -C "$TMP/clone" rev-parse HEAD); inode2=$(ls -di "$TMP/clone/.git/config" | awk '{print $1}'); [ "$sha1" = "$sha2" ] || { echo FAIL_SHA_DIVERGED; exit 1; }; [ "$inode1" = "$inode2" ] || { echo FAIL_INODE_DIVERGED; exit 1; }; echo IDEMPOTENT_OK

steps.name: no GITHUB_TOKEN leaked into .git/config on first clone
steps.cmd:
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d); cat > "$TMPDIR/repos.json" <<CFG
{"repos": [{"owner": "octocat", "name": "Hello-World", "local_path": "/tmp/unused", "enabled": true, "git_url": "https://github.com/octocat/Hello-World.git", "cloud_local_path": "$TMPDIR/clone"}]}
CFG
GITHUB_TOKEN=FAKE_TOKEN_LEAK_SENTINEL HYDRA_REPOS_JSON="$TMPDIR/repos.json" bash scripts/ensure-repo-cloned.sh octocat/Hello-World > /dev/null 2>&1 && ! grep -q FAKE_TOKEN_LEAK_SENTINEL "$TMPDIR/clone/.git/config" && echo NO_TOKEN_LEAK

cases._description: Simulates a worker-implementation whose chat stream dies mid-ticket (18-minute stream-idle timeout — see ticket #45 observed pattern). The fixture would spawn a worker that writes a partial set of files to a worktree, then sleeps past the 12-minute watchdog threshold without ever returning. Commander detects silence + absent completion log, runs scripts/rescue-worker.sh --probe, sees 'rescue-uncommitted' or 'rescue-commits', runs --rescue, then spawns a fresh worker with a resume hint that picks up from the partial work and opens the PR. Ticket #45. Today the runner SKIPs this case — it needs both the worker-subagent executor (same blocker as test-discovery-dormant-to-active, permission-probe, conflict-resolver-additive) AND a timer-mocking primitive so the 12-min wait doesn't actually take 12 minutes on CI.
cases._skip_reason: requires worker-subagent SKIP-path executor + timer mocking; implement when worker-implementation SKIP path lands (ticket #15 follow-up)
cases.ticket_body_summary: A worker-implementation spawned on a fresh ticket goes silent at the 18-minute stream-idle mark after committing partial work. Commander's watchdog (CLAUDE.md 'Worker timeout watchdog') must detect the silence within 12 minutes, run scripts/rescue-worker.sh --probe, run --rescue if probe returns rescue-commits or rescue-uncommitted, then spawn a follow-up worker that finishes the ticket and opens the PR. A 'nothing-to-rescue' probe labels the ticket commander-stuck for operator retry.

must_include_patterns: rescue: partial work from timed-out worker
assertions.expected_artifact: worker opens a draft PR containing both the partial rescued commits and the completion commits; the ticket has a 'commander-rescued' label and a comment listing the rescue commit SHAs

Exercises three guarantees at once: (1) worker physically cannot edit files — after the run, `git status` on the worktree is clean; (2) self-limit holds — even if the fixture contains >3 drifts, the worker files exactly 3; (3) dedup works — if one of the expected findings has a pre-existing open issue whose title substring-matches the candidate, only N-1 issues are filed. SKIPped today, same blocker as the other worker-subagent cases (test-discovery-dormant-to-active, permission-probe, conflict-resolver-additive, worker-timeout-watchdog-rescue) — needs the worker-subagent SKIP-path executor from ticket #15 follow-up. This case documents the expected Q+A shape so the executor has a concrete target when it lands.
cases._skip_reason: requires worker-auditor spawn against a fixture worktree; implement when worker-subagent SKIP-path executor lands (ticket #15 follow-up)
cases.ticket_body_summary: Operator typed `audit`. Commander spawns worker-auditor against the hydra repo. The fixture worktree contains a planted drift: a CLAUDE.md claim with no supporting code, plus two additional drifts that stress the ≤3 cap and dedup logic.

assertions.expected_artifact: worker files between 1 and 3 GitHub issues via `gh issue create`, each labeled commander-ready + commander-auto-filed + tier-<N>, each body rendered from docs/templates/auto-filed-issue.md with all {{PLACEHOLDER}} values substituted. Worktree `git status` is clean after the run — zero files modified. Worker's final report lists the filed issue numbers.

cases._description: Script-only self-test for entrypoint.sh stanzas 5b (repo-clone loop) and 5c (slack bot launch), landed in ticket #80. Uses HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 to exercise the conditional logic without actually cloning or launching anything. Three scenarios via fixture dirs: (1) enabled repo + missing slack.json → logs would-clone + slack-skip; (2) slack.json with enabled=true → logs would-launch-bot; (3) slack.json with enabled=false → logs slack-skip. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-entrypoint-integration.md.

steps.name: dry-run: enabled repo + no slack.json → logs would-clone
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state" && printf '{"repos":[{"owner":"your-org","name":"enabled-repo","enabled":true},{"owner":"your-org","name":"disabled-repo","enabled":false}]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && out=$(HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1) && rm -rf "$TMP" && echo "$out" | grep -F 'DRY RUN: would clone your-org/enabled-repo' && echo FOUND_CLONE

steps.name: dry-run: disabled repo entries are skipped
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state" && printf '{"repos":[{"owner":"your-org","name":"enabled-repo","enabled":true},{"owner":"your-org","name":"disabled-repo","enabled":false}]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && out=$(HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1) && rm -rf "$TMP" && ( echo "$out" | grep -qF 'disabled-repo' && echo BAD_DISABLED_LEAKED || echo DISABLED_SKIPPED )

steps.name: dry-run: missing slack.json → logs slack skip
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state" && printf '{"repos":[]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && out=$(HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1) && rm -rf "$TMP" && echo "$out" | grep -F 'Slack bot:' | grep -F 'skipping' && echo FOUND_SLACK_SKIP

steps.name: dry-run: slack.json enabled=true → logs would-launch
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state/connectors" && printf '{"repos":[]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && printf '{"enabled":true}\n' > "$TMP/state/connectors/slack.json" && out=$(HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1) && rm -rf "$TMP" && echo "$out" | grep -F 'DRY RUN: would launch hydra-slack-bot' && echo FOUND_SLACK_LAUNCH

steps.name: dry-run: slack.json enabled=false → logs skip (not launch)
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state/connectors" && printf '{"repos":[]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && printf '{"enabled":false}\n' > "$TMP/state/connectors/slack.json" && out=$(HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1) && rm -rf "$TMP" && ( echo "$out" | grep -qF 'would launch hydra-slack-bot' && echo BAD_LAUNCH_LEAKED || echo SLACK_DISABLED_SKIPPED )

steps.name: dry-run: exits before tmux with clean status
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$TMP/state" && printf '{"repos":[]}\n' > "$TMP/state/repos.json" && HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 HYDRA_ROOT="$TMP" CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=stub GITHUB_TOKEN=stub HYDRA_MCP_ENABLED=0 HYDRA_DATA_DIR="$TMP/data" HYDRA_HEALTH_PORT=0 bash infra/entrypoint.sh 2>&1 | grep -F 'HYDRA_DRY_RUN=1 — exiting' && rm -rf "$TMP" && echo DRY_RUN_EXIT_LOGGED

cases._description: Script-only self-test for the Slack bot config template (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-slack-bot.md, ticket #74). jq-validates that state/connectors/slack.json.example declares every field bin/hydra-slack-bot reads at startup: enabled (default false), token-env-var pointers, single allowed_user_id, default_channel, poll interval, repos array, seen-PRs cache path. Also smoke-tests the launcher's refuse-to-start paths: missing config, disabled config, and missing env-var tokens — each must exit non-zero (refuse) or zero with a clean log (disabled). No live Slack API test — that would require a real workspace. Runs locally; no worker spawn, no worktree.

steps.name: slack.json.example declares allowed_user_id starting with U
slack.json.example declares repos as an array of owner/name strings

steps.name: bin/hydra-slack-bot refuses to start on missing config

steps.name: bin/hydra-slack-bot exits 0 when enabled=false
steps.cmd: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$tmp/bin" "$tmp/state/connectors" && cp bin/hydra-slack-bot "$tmp/bin/" && echo '{"enabled":false,"bot_token_env":"X","app_token_env":"Y","allowed_user_id":"U1","default_channel":"D1","notification_poll_interval_sec":60,"repos":["a/b"]}' > "$tmp/state/connectors/slack.json" && cd "$tmp" && ./bin/hydra-slack-bot 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 0 ] && echo DISABLED_CLEAN

steps.name: bin/hydra-slack-bot refuses to start when env tokens missing
steps.cmd: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && mkdir -p "$tmp/bin" "$tmp/state/connectors" && cp bin/hydra-slack-bot "$tmp/bin/" && echo '{"enabled":true,"bot_token_env":"HYDRA_SELFTEST_MISSING_BOT","app_token_env":"HYDRA_SELFTEST_MISSING_APP","allowed_user_id":"U1","default_channel":"D1","notification_poll_interval_sec":60,"repos":["a/b"]}' > "$tmp/state/connectors/slack.json" && cd "$tmp" && ./bin/hydra-slack-bot 2>&1; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && echo REFUSED_NO_TOKENS

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-webhook-receiver.py — the GitHub webhook receiver landed in ticket #41. Spins the receiver up in a subprocess on a random free port against a fixture HYDRA_WEBHOOK_SECRET, exercises the HMAC validation (good signature → 200 + sentinel; bad / missing signature → 401), the event-type filter (issues.assigned → sentinel; issues.opened or pull_request → 204 with no sentinel), the sentinel envelope shape, and two security invariants (the request body never lands in stderr; the secret never lands in stderr). cases._description: Fixture case for CLAUDE.md's Scheduled autopickup tick step 1.5 (retro due check). Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-scheduled-retro.md / ticket #85. Exercises four clock scenarios against a fresh state/autopickup.json fixture: (1) last_retro_run=null + Monday 09:05 local fires the retro, (2) last_retro_run=today T09:00:00 + Monday 10:00 local stays silent (idempotent intra-window), (3) last_retro_run=7 days ago + Monday 09:05 local fires (weekly cadence), (4) Monday 08:59 local or Sunday 23:59 local stays silent (time gate). The scheduled-retro logic lives as an inline commander procedure, not as a spawnable helper, so this case needs both a commander-side tick executor AND a clock-mocking primitive (HYDRA_FAKE_NOW=<ISO8601> override) before it can run live. Today it SKIPs under the 'commander-self' kind (matches autopickup-tick-behavior's pattern) but documents the expected behavior for the next executor pass — same pattern as retro-golden-stub, autopickup-tick-behavior, and worker-timeout-watchdog-rescue.
cases._skip_reason: requires commander orchestration + HYDRA_FAKE_NOW clock-mocking primitive; implement when the inline tick executor lands (ticket #85 follow-up)
cases.ticket_body_summary: Each autopickup tick runs a retro-due check: Monday >= 09:00 local AND last_retro_run < today T09:00 -> fire retro, write last_retro_run = now, surface one chat line. Otherwise no-op. Four clock scenarios exercise all branches; the fifth asserts a manual retro in the same Monday window correctly suppresses the scheduled fire.

scenarios.name: manual-retro-earlier-suppresses-scheduled-fire
scenarios._comment: Simulates the manual + scheduled interplay. Operator typed `retro` at 08:30 local (before the 09:00 gate). Manual path wrote last_retro_run = 2026-04-20T08:30:00. When the 09:05 scheduled tick arrives, the idempotency guard reads last_retro_run >= 2026-04-20T09:00:00? No — 08:30 is LESS than 09:00. The fixture therefore MUST still fire, because an early-morning manual run doesn't satisfy the Monday-09:00 semantic. This intentionally captures that the guard is 'after 09:00 today,' not 'any time today.'

assertions.expected_artifact_per_fire: memory/retros/YYYY-WW.md written AND state/autopickup.json:last_retro_run updated to the fake_now_local ISO-8601 string

cases._description: Opt-in self-test for the cloud-worker DinD image variant (ticket #77). SKIPs by default. To run: set HYDRA_TEST_CLOUD_DOCKER=1 on a machine with docker + --privileged support (local laptop with docker desktop works; CI runners generally do NOT). When active, the test (1) builds the image with --build-arg ENABLE_DOCKER=true, (2) confirms docker binary is present + hydra user is in the docker group, (3) runs `docker info` inside the built image under --privileged to verify the inner daemon starts and talks to its socket. Each build is ~400-600 MB + ~60-90s wall-clock, so gating this behind an env var keeps the default CI run fast and prevents false-greens on hosts that can't meaningfully run it. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-cloud-worker-docker.md.

steps.name: build the DinD image variant
steps.cmd: docker build --build-arg ENABLE_DOCKER=true -t hydra-dind-selftest:ci -f infra/Dockerfile . >/tmp/hydra-dind-build-$$.log 2>&1 && echo BUILT_OK || (tail -60 /tmp/hydra-dind-build-$$.log >&2; rm -f /tmp/hydra-dind-build-$$.log; exit 1)

steps.name: docker CLI present in DinD image
steps.cmd: docker run --rm hydra-dind-selftest:ci docker --version | grep -qE 'Docker version' && echo DOCKER_CLI_PRESENT

steps.name: hydra user is in docker group
steps.cmd: docker run --rm hydra-dind-selftest:ci id hydra | grep -qE '\b(docker)\b' && echo HYDRA_IN_DOCKER_GROUP

steps.name: inner dockerd reports a live Server Version under --privileged
steps.cmd: docker run --privileged --rm hydra-dind-selftest:ci sh -c 'dockerd >/tmp/dind.log 2>&1 & for i in $(seq 1 20); do docker info >/tmp/info.log 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done; grep -qE "^\s*Server Version:\s+[0-9]+\." /tmp/info.log && echo DIND_SERVER_UP || (tail -40 /tmp/dind.log /tmp/info.log >&2; exit 1)'

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-mcp-register-agent.sh — the CLI that generates MCP bearer tokens, stores their SHA-256 hash in state/connectors/mcp.json, and prints the raw token once (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-mcp-register-agent.md, ticket #84). Security-adjacent: asserts (a) exit 0 on first-use, (b) stdout token is 43+ chars base64, (c) SHA-256 of the printed token matches the hash in the written config, (d) --rotate produces a different token, preserves scope, keeps a single entry, (e) a non-rotate duplicate call exits 1 with NO config writes (atomicity invariant), plus config-missing auto-bootstrap from mcp.json.example. Fixture lives at self-test/fixtures/mcp-register-agent/run.sh — pattern matches mcp-server-starts. Runs locally in <1s; tmpdir-isolated (HYDRA_MCP_REGISTER_CONFIG points at the tmp path; the real state/connectors/mcp.json is never touched). Commander runs this before committing changes to scripts/hydra-mcp-register-agent.sh or the launcher dispatch in setup.sh.

steps.name: integration smoke (token generation, SHA-256 match, --rotate, duplicate-refusal, bootstrap)
dispatch wired: setup.sh launcher heredoc routes `./hydra mcp register-agent` to the new script
steps.cmd: grep -q 'register-agent) shift; hydra_exec_helper scripts/hydra-mcp-register-agent.sh' setup.sh && echo DISPATCH_WIRED

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py — the outbound MCP client landed in ticket #87. Verifies the 'not-configured' fall-through branch: when state/connectors/mcp.json.example is read (upstream_supervisor.enabled=false by default) OR when the config file is missing entirely, the script exits 2 with a 'not-configured' marker on stdout. Commander reads exit 2 as 'keep legacy commander-stuck behavior, no upstream call made'. Also validates Python syntax, --help output, and that the exit-2 branch never touches the network (no token env var needed to trigger it). Runs locally in <1s; no network, no fixture supervisor required. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-supervisor-escalate-client.md.

steps.name: missing config file → exit 2 + not-configured marker
steps.cmd: out=$(echo '{"worker_id":"w-t","question":"q?"}' | python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --config /nonexistent/mcp.json 2>&1); ec=$?; echo "$out" | grep -q 'not-configured' && [ "$ec" -eq 2 ] && echo DISABLED_OK

steps.name: mcp.json.example (enabled=false) → exit 2 + not-configured marker
steps.cmd: out=$(echo '{"worker_id":"w-t","question":"q?"}' | python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --config state/connectors/mcp.json.example 2>&1); ec=$?; echo "$out" | grep -q 'not-configured' && [ "$ec" -eq 2 ] && echo EXAMPLE_DISABLED_OK

steps.name: exit 2 path writes JSON-parseable stdout
steps.cmd: echo '{"worker_id":"w-t","question":"q?"}' | python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --config state/connectors/mcp.json.example 2>/dev/null | jq -e '.error == "not-configured"' >/dev/null && echo JSON_OK

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py envelope + config validation. Covers the enabled=true branch without making a real network call: uses --check to exercise config parsing, scope enforcement, and envelope shape validation. Verifies (a) valid envelope + valid config + token set → exit 0 with check-ok marker; (b) missing required envelope field → exit 4 + stderr marker; (c) token env var unset → exit 1 + token-missing marker; (d) scope_granted over-broad (anything beyond exactly ['resolve_question']) → exit 1 + scope-overreach marker. All steps stage a fixture mcp.json in a tmpdir — no mutation of the real state/ tree. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-supervisor-escalate-client.md. Related envelope spec: docs/specs/2026-04-16-mcp-agent-interface.md.

steps.name: --check with valid config + token + envelope → exit 0 + check-ok
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; printf '%s' '{"upstream_supervisor":{"enabled":true,"transport":"http","connect_url":"http://127.0.0.1:9999/mcp","auth_token_env":"TEST_SUP_TOKEN","scope_granted":["resolve_question"],"timeout_sec":5}}' > "$TMP/mcp.json"; out=$(echo '{"worker_id":"w-1","question":"q?"}' | TEST_SUP_TOKEN=x python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --check --config "$TMP/mcp.json" 2>&1); ec=$?; echo "$out" | grep -q 'check-ok' && [ "$ec" -eq 0 ] && echo CHECK_OK

steps.name: --check with malformed envelope (missing worker_id) → exit 4
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d) && trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; printf '%s' '{"upstream_supervisor":{"enabled":true,"transport":"http","connect_url":"http://127.0.0.1:9999/mcp","auth_token_env":"TEST_SUP_TOKEN","scope_granted":["resolve_question"],"timeout_sec":5}}' > "$TMP/mcp.json"; err=$(echo '{"question":"missing worker_id"}' | TEST_SUP_TOKEN=x python3 scripts/hydra-supervisor-escalate.py --check --config "$TMP/mcp.json" 2>&1 >/dev/null); ec=$?; echo "$err" | grep -q 'missing required field' && [ "$ec" -eq 4 ] && echo SHAPE_REJECTED

steps.name: enabled config + no token env var → exit 1 + token-missing

cases._description: Script-only self-test for the asciinema demo artifact shipped in ticket #37 (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-asciinema-demo.md). Verifies (a) scripts/record-demo.sh is syntactically valid bash and responds to --help; (b) scripts/record-demo.sh --dry-run exits 0 without launching asciinema; (c) docs/demo/demo.cast exists and its first line is valid asciinema v2 JSON (.version == 2); (d) every subsequent line parses as a 3-element JSON array [timestamp, event_type, data]; (e) when the header .title contains the string 'placeholder' (case-insensitive), the stricter event-count check SKIPs — placeholder casts are intentionally minimal, and failing CI on a placeholder branch would block every other PR until someone re-records. Once an operator ships a real cast (title without 'placeholder'), the event-count floor kicks in automatically.

steps.name: scripts/record-demo.sh --dry-run --no-preflight exits 0 without starting asciinema
steps.cmd: NO_COLOR=1 scripts/record-demo.sh --dry-run --no-preflight > /tmp/hydra-demo-dry.out 2>&1 && grep -q 'dry-run complete' /tmp/hydra-demo-dry.out && ! grep -q 'starting asciinema rec' /tmp/hydra-demo-dry.out && echo DRY_RUN_OK

steps.name: first line of docs/demo/demo.cast is valid asciinema v2 header
steps.cmd: head -1 docs/demo/demo.cast | jq -e '.version == 2 and (.width | type) == "number" and (.height | type) == "number"' > /dev/null && echo HEADER_OK

steps.name: every event line in docs/demo/demo.cast parses as [number, string, string]
steps.cmd: total=$(wc -l < docs/demo/demo.cast); bad=0; for i in $(seq 2 $total); do sed -n "${i}p" docs/demo/demo.cast | jq -e 'type == "array" and length == 3 and (.[0]|type) == "number" and (.[1]|type) == "string" and (.[2]|type) == "string"' > /dev/null || bad=1; done; [ $bad -eq 0 ] && echo EVENTS_OK

steps.name: stricter content checks: SKIP on placeholder, enforce min-event-count on real casts
steps.cmd: if head -1 docs/demo/demo.cast | jq -e '.title // "" | test("placeholder"; "i")' > /dev/null; then echo 'PLACEHOLDER_SKIP (ok — skipping event-count floor)'; else events=$(($(wc -l < docs/demo/demo.cast) - 1)); [ "$events" -ge 20 ] && echo 'REAL_CAST_OK' || { echo "FAIL: real cast has only $events events, need >= 20" >&2; exit 1; }; fi

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-codex-exec.sh (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-codex-worker-backend.md, ticket #96). The stub approach keeps the test deterministic and free (no real Codex quota burn). Operators with a working local codex env can set HYDRA_TEST_CODEX_E2E=1 to additionally smoke --check against the real binary; the wrapper's full control flow is still exercised by the stubs regardless.

steps.name: codex-worker fixture roundtrip (13 stubbed assertions + prompt-leak guard)

cases._description: Exercises the --json output paths on ./hydra status and ./hydra ps against deterministic fixtures and the canonical schema at state/schemas/hydra-status-output.schema.json. Hard-fails if either subcommand emits malformed JSON, drifts from the required top-level shape, or breaks the stable `version` contract. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-json-output-mode.md (ticket #105). Runs locally; no network, no worker spawn, no Claude auth.

steps.name: bash -n clean on hydra-status.sh and hydra-ps.sh

steps.name: schema file is valid JSON and advertises version 1.0

steps.name: status --json autopickup block reflects fixture values

cases._description: Script-only self-test for the interactive setup.sh rewrite (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-interactive-setup.md, ticket #102). Verifies that (a) --non-interactive is accepted, (b) --ci is a working alias, (c) stdin-not-a-TTY auto-triggers the non-interactive path without hanging on `read`, (d) the .hydra.env format contract survives (all three required exports present in the right order), (e) no prompt text leaks to stdout in non-interactive mode, (f) --help prints usage without running setup, and (g) unknown flags exit 2. Each step stages a tmp HYDRA_ROOT, runs setup.sh once there, and cleans up — the live worktree is never touched. Requires claude + gh just like setup.sh itself; SKIPs cleanly on CI runners without those tools (same pattern as autopickup-default-on-smoke).

steps.name: setup.sh --help prints usage without running setup
setup.sh --bogus exits 2 with actionable hint
steps.cmd: NO_COLOR=1 ./setup.sh --bogus 2>&1; rc=$?; [ "$rc" = "2" ] && echo BAD_FLAG_REJECTED || { echo "FAIL rc=$rc" >&2; exit 1; }

steps.name: setup.sh --non-interactive in a tmp HYDRA_ROOT exits 0 and writes .hydra.env
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; mkdir -p "$TMP/.claude" "$TMP/state"; cp setup.sh "$TMP/"; cp .claude/settings.local.json.example "$TMP/.claude/"; cp state/repos.example.json "$TMP/state/"; chmod +x "$TMP/setup.sh"; ( cd "$TMP" && env -u HYDRA_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_DIR -u HYDRA_ROOT -u COMMANDER_ROOT NO_COLOR=1 ./setup.sh --non-interactive > /tmp/hydra-102-ni.out 2>&1 ); rc=$?; [ "$rc" = "0" ] && [ -f "$TMP/.hydra.env" ] && echo NI_EXIT0_ENV_WRITTEN || { echo "FAIL rc=$rc" >&2; cat /tmp/hydra-102-ni.out >&2; exit 1; }

steps.name: setup.sh --ci is accepted as alias
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; mkdir -p "$TMP/.claude" "$TMP/state"; cp setup.sh "$TMP/"; cp .claude/settings.local.json.example "$TMP/.claude/"; cp state/repos.example.json "$TMP/state/"; chmod +x "$TMP/setup.sh"; ( cd "$TMP" && env -u HYDRA_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_DIR -u HYDRA_ROOT -u COMMANDER_ROOT NO_COLOR=1 ./setup.sh --ci > /dev/null 2>&1 ); rc=$?; [ "$rc" = "0" ] && [ -f "$TMP/.hydra.env" ] && echo CI_ALIAS_OK || { echo "FAIL rc=$rc" >&2; exit 1; }

steps.name: setup.sh with closed stdin (</dev/null) auto-triggers non-interactive path (no read hang, no set -e trip)
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; mkdir -p "$TMP/.claude" "$TMP/state"; cp setup.sh "$TMP/"; cp .claude/settings.local.json.example "$TMP/.claude/"; cp state/repos.example.json "$TMP/state/"; chmod +x "$TMP/setup.sh"; ( cd "$TMP" && env -u HYDRA_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_DIR -u HYDRA_ROOT -u COMMANDER_ROOT NO_COLOR=1 ./setup.sh </dev/null > /tmp/hydra-102-devnull.out 2>&1 ); rc=$?; [ "$rc" = "0" ] && [ -f "$TMP/.hydra.env" ] && echo CLOSED_STDIN_OK || { echo "FAIL rc=$rc" >&2; cat /tmp/hydra-102-devnull.out >&2; exit 1; }

steps.name: non-interactive .hydra.env has all three exports in the contracted order
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; mkdir -p "$TMP/.claude" "$TMP/state"; cp setup.sh "$TMP/"; cp .claude/settings.local.json.example "$TMP/.claude/"; cp state/repos.example.json "$TMP/state/"; chmod +x "$TMP/setup.sh"; ( cd "$TMP" && env -u HYDRA_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_DIR -u HYDRA_ROOT -u COMMANDER_ROOT NO_COLOR=1 ./setup.sh --non-interactive > /dev/null 2>&1 ); grep -q '^export HYDRA_ROOT=' "$TMP/.hydra.env" && grep -q '^export HYDRA_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_DIR=' "$TMP/.hydra.env" && grep -q '^export COMMANDER_ROOT=' "$TMP/.hydra.env" && awk 'NR==3{if($0 ~ /^export HYDRA_ROOT=/) a=1} NR==4{if($0 ~ /^export HYDRA_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_DIR=/) b=1} NR==5{if($0 ~ /^export COMMANDER_ROOT=/) c=1} END{exit !(a && b && c)}' "$TMP/.hydra.env" && echo ENV_FORMAT_OK || { echo FAIL >&2; cat "$TMP/.hydra.env" >&2; exit 1; }

steps.name: non-interactive mode does NOT leak prompt text ('Press Enter') to stdout
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; mkdir -p "$TMP/.claude" "$TMP/state"; cp setup.sh "$TMP/"; cp .claude/settings.local.json.example "$TMP/.claude/"; cp state/repos.example.json "$TMP/state/"; chmod +x "$TMP/setup.sh"; ( cd "$TMP" && env -u HYDRA_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_DIR -u HYDRA_ROOT -u COMMANDER_ROOT NO_COLOR=1 ./setup.sh --non-interactive > /tmp/hydra-102-noleak.out 2>&1 ); ! grep -q 'Press Enter' /tmp/hydra-102-noleak.out && echo NO_PROMPT_LEAK || { echo "FAIL: prompt text leaked" >&2; grep 'Press Enter' /tmp/hydra-102-noleak.out >&2; exit 1; }

steps.name: second run on a populated tree is a no-op byte-identical to the first
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; mkdir -p "$TMP/.claude" "$TMP/state"; cp setup.sh "$TMP/"; cp .claude/settings.local.json.example "$TMP/.claude/"; cp state/repos.example.json "$TMP/state/"; chmod +x "$TMP/setup.sh"; ( cd "$TMP" && env -u HYDRA_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_DIR -u HYDRA_ROOT -u COMMANDER_ROOT NO_COLOR=1 ./setup.sh --non-interactive > /dev/null 2>&1 ); cp "$TMP/.hydra.env" "$TMP/.env.first"; cp "$TMP/hydra" "$TMP/hydra.first"; ( cd "$TMP" && env -u HYDRA_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_DIR -u HYDRA_ROOT -u COMMANDER_ROOT NO_COLOR=1 ./setup.sh --non-interactive > /dev/null 2>&1 ); diff -q "$TMP/.env.first" "$TMP/.hydra.env" > /dev/null && diff -q "$TMP/hydra.first" "$TMP/hydra" > /dev/null && echo IDEMPOTENT_OK || { echo FAIL >&2; exit 1; }

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-doctor.sh --fix mode (ticket #104, spec docs/specs/2026-04-17-doctor-fix-mode.md). Runs doctor against a deliberately-broken fixture (self-test/fixtures/hydra-doctor/broken/) with HYDRA_ROOT overridden, and asserts (a) every ✗ / ⚠ line is immediately followed by an actionable hint prefixed fix:, run:, or manual:; (b) --fix-safe creates the missing logs/ directory (backward-compat with ticket #36's --fix-safe path); (c) --help advertises --fix; (d) --fix with stdin closed and no /dev/tty doesn't crash — the walkthrough aborts gracefully. Skipped categories: environment/integrations/self-test (they inspect the host, not HYDRA_ROOT). Any new check added to hydra-doctor without a fix_hint will cause step (a) to fail — that's the intended regression gate.

steps.name: config category against broken fixture → every ✗ has a hint
steps.cmd: OUT=$(HYDRA_ROOT=self-test/fixtures/hydra-doctor/broken NO_COLOR=1 scripts/hydra-doctor.sh --category=config 2>&1 || true); fail_count=$(printf '%s\n' "$OUT" | grep -c '^  ✗ ' || true); hint_count=$(printf '%s\n' "$OUT" | grep -cE '^      (fix|run|manual): ' || true); [ "$fail_count" -ge 2 ] && [ "$fail_count" -eq "$hint_count" ] && echo "HINTS_OK fails=$fail_count hints=$hint_count"

steps.name: runtime category against broken fixture → every ✗/⚠ has a hint
steps.cmd: OUT=$(HYDRA_ROOT=self-test/fixtures/hydra-doctor/broken NO_COLOR=1 scripts/hydra-doctor.sh --category=runtime 2>&1 || true); bad_count=$(printf '%s\n' "$OUT" | grep -cE '^  (✗|⚠) ' || true); hint_count=$(printf '%s\n' "$OUT" | grep -cE '^      (fix|run|manual): ' || true); [ "$bad_count" -ge 3 ] && [ "$bad_count" -eq "$hint_count" ] && echo "HINTS_OK bad=$bad_count hints=$hint_count"

steps.name: --fix-safe against fixture creates missing logs/ (backward-compat with #36)
steps.cmd: rm -rf self-test/fixtures/hydra-doctor/broken/logs; HYDRA_ROOT=self-test/fixtures/hydra-doctor/broken NO_COLOR=1 scripts/hydra-doctor.sh --category=runtime --fix-safe >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; [ -d self-test/fixtures/hydra-doctor/broken/logs ] && echo FIX_SAFE_OK; rm -rf self-test/fixtures/hydra-doctor/broken/logs

steps.name: --fix with stdin closed ends walkthrough gracefully (no bash abort)
steps.cmd: HYDRA_ROOT=self-test/fixtures/hydra-doctor/broken NO_COLOR=1 scripts/hydra-doctor.sh --category=config --fix </dev/null >/tmp/hydra-doctor-fix.out 2>&1 || true; grep -q 'Summary:' /tmp/hydra-doctor-fix.out && echo FIX_NONINTERACTIVE_OK; rm -f /tmp/hydra-doctor-fix.out

cases._description: Script-only self-test for bot-account mode — the per-repo assignee_override field added in ticket #26 (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-assignee-override.md). Exercises: (1) the patched state/schemas/repos.schema.json still validates a fixture WITH the new field and WITHOUT it (back-compat), (2) scripts/hydra-add-repo.sh --help documents --assignee and --state-file, (3) the CLI writes assignee_override when --assignee is given, (4) the CLI omits the field when --assignee is absent, (5) the CLI rejects empty and invalid override values with exit 2 before touching state. No worker spawn, no Claude auth, no network. Uses --state-file to avoid mutating the worktree's committed state/repos.json. Runs on every CI tick.

steps.name: back-compat (no override) fixture is valid JSON
positive fixture passes validate-state.sh against patched schema

schema declares the assignee_override property (type, minLength, pattern)

steps.name: --assignee with bad value exits 2 without writing

steps.name: --assignee hydra-bot-foo writes the field
steps.cmd: TMP=$(mktemp -d); cp self-test/fixtures/assignee-override/empty-repos.json "$TMP/repos.json"; mkdir "$TMP/fake-clone"; ( cd "$TMP/fake-clone" && git init -q ); NO_COLOR=1 scripts/hydra-add-repo.sh foo/bar --local-path "$TMP/fake-clone" --trigger assignee --assignee hydra-bot-foo --non-interactive --state-file "$TMP/repos.json" >/dev/null; got=$(jq -r '.repos[0].assignee_override' "$TMP/repos.json"); rm -rf "$TMP"; [ "$got" = "hydra-bot-foo" ] && echo WRITE_OK

steps.name: --assignee with [bot] suffix is accepted

cases._description: Script-only self-test for scripts/hydra-migrate-to-cloud.sh — the migration ritual shipped in ticket #106 (spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-cloud-default-oauth.md). Verifies: (a) script is bash-syntax-clean; (b) --help prints the expected usage block; (c) --dry-run --non-interactive (with fake creds exported) completes successfully without invoking any real fly / claude / gh / ./hydra binary — tested by staging a tmpdir PATH that makes those binaries fail loudly if called; (d) dry-run output lists the exact set of Fly secret NAMES that would be staged, and does NOT print secret VALUES. This is the regression gate for the dual-OAuth migration flow — if a future refactor starts leaking values or shelling out in dry-run, this case fails.

steps.name: --dry-run --non-interactive completes with fake creds and lists secret names
steps.cmd: out=$(HYDRA_FLY_APP=test-fixture-app CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=sk-ant-oat-fake GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_fake OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-fake NO_COLOR=1 scripts/hydra-migrate-to-cloud.sh --dry-run --non-interactive 2>&1); ec=$?; echo "$out" | grep -q 'Dry-run complete' && echo "$out" | grep -q 'would stage CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN' && echo "$out" | grep -q 'would stage OPENAI_API_KEY' && echo "$out" | grep -q 'would stage GITHUB_TOKEN' && [ "$ec" -eq 0 ] && echo DRY_RUN_OK

steps.name: --dry-run does NOT leak secret values into output
steps.cmd: out=$(HYDRA_FLY_APP=test-fixture-app CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=sk-ant-oat-SECRETMARKER GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_SECRETMARKER OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-SECRETMARKER NO_COLOR=1 scripts/hydra-migrate-to-cloud.sh --dry-run --non-interactive 2>&1); ! echo "$out" | grep -q 'SECRETMARKER' && echo NO_LEAK

steps.name: --dry-run with stubbed PATH does not invoke real fly / claude / gh
steps.cmd:
TMP=$(mktemp -d) && trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT; for bin in fly flyctl claude gh codex; do cat > "$TMP/$bin" <<'STUB'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
echo "REAL_$(basename "$0")_CALLED" >&2
